source.title: The Child Tax Credit, decoded: who qualifies and what's refundable

ad_headlines: A credit per qualifying child. Part of it can come back as a refund.
Schedule 8812 isn't optional paperwork — it's where the credit lives.
Five qualifying-child tests. One credit. Worth checking every year.
variants.twitter:
The Child Tax Credit: a per-child credit that directly reduces tax owed, with part of it potentially refundable through the Additional Child Tax Credit.

Per-child amount + phase-outs are set by law and have changed multiple times.

Source: IRC §24, Schedule 8812. Educational.
variants.linkedin:
The Child Tax Credit is one of the most-changed lines in the U.S. tax code. The mechanic, however, is stable.

Per IRC §24 and Schedule 8812 of Form 1040:

• Each qualifying child generates a credit that directly reduces tax owed.
• A portion is refundable through the Additional Child Tax Credit — families can receive value even if it exceeds the tax they owe.
• A 'qualifying child' has to clear five IRS tests: relationship, age, residency, support, and a valid Social Security number.
• The credit phases out above income thresholds tied to filing status.

What moves between years: the per-child amount, the age threshold, and the phase-out ranges. Always check current IRS guidance for the tax year — last year's number isn't this year's.

Where to claim it: Schedule 8812 attached to your 1040. The credit isn't automatic — the form is the mechanism.

Educational only. Not tax advice — verify current-year per-child amount, age limit, and phase-outs against IRS guidance.
variants.reddit:
**Child Tax Credit, decoded — what's refundable vs. what isn't**

Per IRC §24 and Schedule 8812:

- Each qualifying child triggers a credit that reduces tax owed dollar for dollar.
- A portion is refundable through the Additional Child Tax Credit — meaning if the credit exceeds your tax owed, you can still receive value back.
- A 'qualifying child' has to pass IRS tests for relationship, age, residency, support, and must have a valid SSN.
- Income phase-outs kick in above certain thresholds based on filing status.

The per-child amount, the age cap, and the phase-out numbers have all changed multiple times over the last several years. Always verify the current-year rule before you file.

Mechanism: Schedule 8812 attached to Form 1040. Without that form, no credit.

Sources: IRC §24, Schedule 8812. Educational only — not advice.

brand_voice_principles: Plain-spoken, confident, demystifying. Translates tax-code complexity.
A little irreverent about how needlessly complicated the system is — on the reader's side.
Specific and sourced. Every claim points to a real rule.
No: 'one weird trick', 'IRS hates this', guaranteed savings, sleaze.
